classification.core_constraint: canonical law can still become temporally misaligned with execution state under load
classification.authority_gap: enforcement boundary exists conceptually but synchronization between law version and commit context remains vulnerable

recommended_message: Terry — this tracks very cleanly. The custody boundary framing and the shift from evaluation to prevention is exactly where the system becomes real. The only place I’ve seen this still degrade is when the law layer remains logically external, but temporally behind execution. At that point, the system is resolving against a canonical layer that is already stale relative to the state it’s governing. So even though enforcement is happening at the boundary, it’s enforcing yesterday’s authority against today’s execution. Curious whether you’ve seen that failure mode—where enforcement is correct, but temporally misaligned.
